denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/06/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102961706          Group #: GRP-1374
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-155259
Claim #: CLM-2026-207823
Date of Service (requested): 01/17/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 01/19/2026 for Parathyroidectomy related to Primary hyperparathyroidism (E21.0). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Parathyroidectomy
Diagnosis (as submitted): Primary hyperparathyroidism (E21.0)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0114 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0114 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Continuation of Parathyroidectomy requires an active prior authorization; the prior auth lapsed. A new authorization must be submitted.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with continuation of care lapsed. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102961706) and reference PA-2026-155259. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department

This determination was made by Dr. J. Smith, Medical Director.
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Primary hyperparathyroidism (E21.0). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Parathyroidectomy recommended as the next appropriate step. Calcium 11.4 mg/dL, PTH 128 pg/mL, kidney stones in 2024. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that continuation of care lapsed bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
